session.user:
Last login: Fri Mar 6 14:19:41 on ttys005 direnv: loading ~/code/erk/.envrc direnv: loading ~/code/erk/.erk/bin/activate.sh -> cd /Users/schrockn/code/erk -> Activating venv: /Users/schrockn/code/erk/.venv (3.13.1) Activated: /Users/schrockn/code/erk OK workspace:78 direnv: export +VIRTUAL_ENV +VIRTUAL_ENV_PROMPT ~PATH ➜ erk git:(master) erk pr teleport 8855 Fetching PR #8855... Teleported plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330 to match remote (PR #8855) ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) make clear_impl_context rm -rf .impl rm -rf .erk/impl-context ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) ✗ git diff ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) ✗ gits t zsh: command not found: gits ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) ✗ git commit -a -m cp [plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330 cb23a6ca0] cp 5 files changed, 67 deletions(-) delete mode 100644 .erk/impl-context/plan.md delete mode 100644 .erk/impl-context/ref.json delete mode 100644 .impl/issue.json delete mode 100644 .impl/local-run-state.json delete mode 100644 .impl/plan.md ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) gt submit ERROR: Cannot perform this operation on untracked branch plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330. You can track it by specifying its parent with gt track. ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) gt track master is already tracked. Tracked branch plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330 with parent master (includes 8 commits). ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) gt submit 🥞 Validating that this Graphite stack is ready to submit...
ERROR: Branch plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330 has been updated remotely. Use gt get or gt sync to sync with remote before submitting (or use the --force flag to override this check). ➜ erk git:(plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) gt get 🌲 Fetching branches from remote... Running git fetch: [========================================] 100% | Done master is up to date.
🔄 Syncing branches... ? Branch plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330 exists locally, but has never been submitted or synced with Graphite. Overwrite it with the remote version? › (Y/n)
i thought teleport was already suppose to get the remote version. what happened ehre?
session.assistant:
The issue is a mismatch between git state and Graphite's internal tracking:
1. `erk pr teleport` synced the **git branch** to match remote — that worked fine 2. But Graphite has its own internal metadata (separate from git). Since this branch was created outside of `gt`, Graphite doesn't know about it 3. When you ran `gt track`, Graphite started tracking it but still sees a discrepancy between its metadata and the remote PR state 4. Then your local `cp` commit (deleting impl-context files) made local diverge further from remote
The `gt get` prompt is Graphite asking: "I've never synced this branch — should I overwrite your local with what's on GitHub?" Since you have a local commit (`cp`) that isn't on remote, answering Y would lose that commit.
**What to do now:** Answer **N** to keep your local commit, then force submit:
``` gt submit --no-interactive --force ```
The `--force` flag overrides the "updated remotely" check, and since your local already includes the remote content (via teleport) plus your cleanup commit, this is safe.
**Re: teleport** — teleport does its job (syncing git), but it can't register the branch with Graphite's internal tracking system. That's a `gt` concern. You might want to consider having teleport also run `gt track` automatically if it doesn't already.
session.user: yes i want you to investigate, find the root cause, and plan a fix
session.assistant: Let me investigate the teleport command and understand the flow.
session.tool_result: Found 8 files src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/setup_impl_from_pr.py src/erk/cli/commands/pr/__init__.py src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py src/erk/tui/actions/palette.py src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py src/erk/cli/commands/pr/teleport_cmd.py src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/get_pr_body_footer.py

Found 9 files CHANGELOG.md .claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md docs/learned/planning/tripwires.md docs/learned/planning/lifecycle.md docs/learned/planning/index.md docs/learned/planning/stacked-plan-branch-rebase.md docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-branch-teleport.md docs/learned/planning/planned-pr-lifecycle.md docs/learned/architecture/derived-flags.md

1→"""Teleport a PR's remote state to the local branch. 2→ 3→Remote-first counterpart to 'checkout'. While checkout preserves local state 4→(reusing existing worktrees, keeping unpushed commits), teleport force-resets 5→the local branch to match the remote exactly. Use teleport when a remote agent 6→has pushed commits and you want your local copy to match. 7→ 8→Operates on the current worktree by default, with --new-slot to create a fresh 9→worktree slot. 10→""" 11→ 12→from pathlib import Path 13→ 14→import click 15→ 16→from erk.cli.activation import ( 17→ activation_config_activate_only, 18→ ensure_worktree_activate_script, 19→ print_activation_instructions, 20→) 21→from erk.cli.commands.checkout_helpers import ensure_branch_has_worktree 22→from erk.cli.commands.pr.checkout_cmd import _fetch_and_update_branch 23→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 24→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 25→from erk.core.repo_discovery import NoRepoSentinel, RepoContext 26→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound 27→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 28→ 29→ 30→@click.command("teleport")
... [241 lines omitted] ...
57→ ctx.console.error("Not in a git repository") 65→ ctx.console.error( 72→ ctx.console.error("Teleport is not supported for cross-repository (fork) PRs.") 246→ except RuntimeError: 261→ click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow")

1→--- 2→title: Planned PR Branch Teleport 3→read_when: 4→ - "implementing or debugging planned-PR plan setup" 5→ - "understanding branch teleport during plan implementation" 6→ - "working with setup_impl_from_pr for planned PR plans" 7→ - "debugging divergence between local and remote plan branches" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "implementing planned-PR plan without teleporting from remote" 10→ warning: "Before implementing a planned-PR plan, always teleport from remote: fetch_branch -> checkout/create_tracking -> pull_rebase" 11→ - action: "detecting plan backend by checking backend type directly" 12→ warning: "Use github.get_pr() + pr_result.head_ref_name to discover the plan branch. There is only one backend (planned-PR)." 13→ - action: "creating a new branch for a planned-PR plan" 14→ warning: "Planned PR plans already have a branch created during plan-save. Reuse the existing branch, don't create a new one." 15→ - action: "committing to planned-PR plan branches after checkout without pulling remote" 16→ warning: "Both setup_impl_from_pr.py and submit.py use the same three-step teleport: fetch_branch -> checkout/create_tracking -> pull_rebase. Skipping pull_rebase causes non-fast-forward push failures." 17→--- 18→ 19→# Planned PR Branch Teleport 20→ 21→When implementing a planned-PR plan, the local branch must be teleported from remote because plan-save creates the branch and pushes it, then checks out the original branch. Remote may receive additional commits before implementation runs. 22→ 23→## The Problem 24→ 25→1. `plan_save` creates a branch, pushes it to remote, creates draft PR 26→2. `plan_save` checks out the original branch (user continues working) 27→3. Time passes (remote CI may add commits, user may re-plan) 28→4. `plan-implement` runs and needs the latest branch state 29→ 30→## Three-Step Teleport Sequence
... [79 lines omitted] ...
87→PR #7697 added the missing `pull_rebase()` call to the submit path. Without it, the submit path would attempt to push commits onto a branch that had diverged from remote, causing non-fast-forward push failures.
session.assistant: Found the root cause. Let me verify one more thing.
session.tool_result: src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py:132: ctx.branch_manager.track_branch(repo_root, branch, trunk_branch) src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py:400: ctx.branch_manager.track_branch(repo_root, branch, parent_branch) src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py:544: # Defer track_branch until after worktree is created — for stacked plans src/erk/cli/commands/branch/create_cmd.py:178: ctx.branch_manager.track_branch(repo.root, branch_name, trunk) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/teleport_cmd.py:234: ctx.branch_manager.track_branch(repo.root, branch_name, base_ref_name) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/teleport_cmd.py:239: ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo.root, branch_name) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py:262: ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo.root, branch_name) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/checkout_cmd.py:303: ctx.branch_manager.track_branch(repo.root, branch_name, pr.base_ref_name) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rewrite_cmd.py:152: ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(discovery.repo_root, discovery.current_branch) src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:829: ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(state.repo_root, state.branch_name) src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py:260: branch_manager.retrack_branch(repo_root, branch_name)
session.assistant:
**Root cause found.** In `teleport_cmd.py:198-214`, `_reconstruct_graphite_stack` has an early return:
```python def _reconstruct_graphite_stack(...): trunk = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root) if base_ref_name == trunk: return # Not a stacked PR <-- BUG: skips ALL Graphite tracking ```
For non-stacked PRs (base is `master`), the function returns immediately **without registering the branch with Graphite at all**. This means after teleport, the git branch is correct but Graphite has no metadata for it.
Compare with `checkout_cmd.py:303` which correctly calls `track_branch` for non-stacked PRs too: ```python ctx.branch_manager.track_branch(repo.root, branch_name, pr.base_ref_name) ```
**The fix:** `_reconstruct_graphite_stack` should also track/retrack the branch when `base_ref_name == trunk`, not skip it entirely. The function name is misleading — it says "reconstruct stack" implying it's only for stacked PRs, but even a single branch needs Graphite registration.
**Plan:**
1. **Rename** `_reconstruct_graphite_stack` → `_register_with_graphite` (it's not just for stacks) 2. **Remove** the early return on `base_ref_name == trunk` 3. **Keep** the `is_graphite_managed()` guard (still correct — skip if Graphite not enabled) 4. **Keep** the base-branch-fetching logic gated on `base_ref_name != trunk` (only needed for stacks) 5. **Always** run the track/retrack logic at lines 230-239
The restructured function would look like: ```python def _register_with_graphite(...): if not ctx.branch_manager.is_graphite_managed(): return
trunk = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
# For stacked PRs, fetch base branch if needed if base_ref_name != trunk: local_branches = ctx.git.branch.list_local_branches(repo.root) if base_ref_name not in local_branches: ctx.console.info(f"Fetching base branch '{base_ref_name}'...") ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(repo.root, "origin", base_ref_name) ctx.branch_manager.create_tracking_branch(...)
# Always register with Graphite (stacked or not) parent = ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(repo.root, branch_name) if parent is None: ctx.branch_manager.track_branch(repo.root, branch_name, base_ref_name) else: if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None: ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo.root, branch_name) ```
Want me to implement this fix?

tool_use.name: Write
tool_use.param:
# Fix: `erk pr teleport` doesn't register branch with Graphite for non-stacked PRs
## Con...[truncated 2051 chars]...ere are tests covering the Graphite registration path for non-stacked PRs; add one if not

PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Fix: `erk pr teleport` doesn't register branch with Graphite for non-stacked PRs Current context: (wt:erk) (br:plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) (pr:#8855)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:plnd/fix-"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id 9a1e2cd7-69b6-4bbf-849d-4ed8836cf6e9 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch. Read the plan from: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/memoized-noodling-lerdorf.md Implement changes, run CI, and optionally 'erk pr submit' when done.
... [6 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
# Fix: `erk pr teleport` doesn't register branch with Graphite for non-stacked PRs
## Context
After `erk pr teleport`, running `gt submit` fails because Graphite doesn't know about the branch. The user must manually run `gt track` and `gt get` to sync state. Teleport should leave the branch fully ready for Graphite operations.
**Root cause:** `_reconstruct_graphite_stack` in `teleport_cmd.py:213` has an early return when `base_ref_name == trunk`, skipping ALL Graphite registration for non-stacked PRs. Compare with `checkout_cmd.py:303` which correctly calls `track_branch` for all PRs.
## Changes
### File: `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/teleport_cmd.py`
1. **Rename** `_reconstruct_graphite_stack` → `_register_with_graphite` 2. **Restructure** to always run track/retrack, gating only the base-branch-fetch on stacked PRs 3. **Update** both call sites (lines 151, 183) to use the new name
## Verification
- Run existing teleport tests to ensure no regressions - Check if there are tests covering the Graphite registration path for non-stacked PRs; add one if not

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': "Fix: `erk pr teleport` doesn't register branch with Graphite for non-stacke...[truncated 454 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result:
User has answered your questions: "Fix: `erk pr teleport` doesn't register branch with Graphite for non-stacked PRs Current context: (wt:erk) (br:plnd/fix-learn-pr-dry-violation-03-06-1330) (pr:#8855)
What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.assistant: User chose to save as a planned PR. Let me run the plan-save skill.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="9a1e2cd7-69b6-4bbf-849d-4ed8836cf6e9" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="9a1e2cd7-69b6-4bbf-849d-4ed8836cf6e9" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "9a1e2cd7-69b6-4bbf-849d-4ed8836cf6e9" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "9a1e2cd7-69b6-4bbf-849d-4ed8836cf6e9" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "9a1e2cd7-69b6-4bbf-849d-4ed8836cf6e9" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "9a1e2cd7-69b6-4bbf-849d-4ed8836cf6e9" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant:
Let me follow the plan-save steps.
**Step 1:** No special flags.
**Step 2:** Branch slug from title "Fix: `erk pr teleport` doesn't register branch with Graphite for non-stacked PRs" → `fix-teleport-graphite-track`
**Step 3:** Summary: "Restructures _reconstruct_graphite_stack in teleport_cmd.py to always register branches with Graphite after teleport. Currently the function early-returns for non-stacked PRs (base == trunk), leaving the branch untracked by Graphite and causing gt submit failures."
**Step 4:** Running the save command.
I need to save the plan first, then exit plan mode. Let me run the save command.

tool_use.name: Bash

Save plan as draft PR via erk plan-save

session.assistant: Plan saved. Now pushing the planning session.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Push planning session for cross-machine learning

session.assistant: Plan **"Fix: `erk pr teleport` doesn't register branch with Graphite for non-stacked PRs"** saved as draft PR **#8863**. URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8863

tool_use.param:

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: Plan #8863 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
Implement plan #8863: In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8863 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8863 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8863 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8863 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout plan #8863: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8863 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8863
Dispatch plan #8863: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8863 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch
Session complete. Do NOT call ExitPlanMode again.
session.assistant:
Plan #8863 saved. Here are your next steps:
**Implement plan #8863:** ``` In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8863 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8863 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8863 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8863 --script)" && erk implement -d ```
**Checkout plan #8863:** ``` In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8863 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8863 ```
**Dispatch plan #8863:** ``` CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8863 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch ```
session.user:
# /erk:pr-dispatch
## Goal
Find the most recent plan created in this conversation and dispatch it for remote AI implementation via `erk pr dispatch`.
## What This Command Does
1. Search conversation for the last plan reference 2. Extract the plan number 3. Run `erk pr dispatch <plan_number>` to trigger remote implementation
## Finding the Plan
Search the conversation from bottom to top for these patterns (in priority order):
1. **Draft PR reference**: `saved as draft PR #<number>` or `draft PR #<number>` 2. **Pull request URL**: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<number>` 3. **Plan URL**: `https://github.com/.../pull/<number>` (legacy: `https://github.com/.../issues/<number>`)
Extract the plan number from the most recent match.
## Execution
Once you have the plan number, run:
```bash erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If the command succeeds, clear the session marker to allow creating new plans in this session:
```bash erk exec marker delete --session-id "9a1e2cd7-69b6-4bbf-849d-4ed8836cf6e9" plan-saved-issue ```
Display the command output to the user. The `erk pr dispatch` command handles all validation (plan existence, labels, state).
## Error Cases
- **No plan found in conversation**: Report "No plan found in conversation. Run /erk:plan-save first to create a plan." - **erk pr dispatch fails**: Display the error output from the command (erk pr dispatch validates the plan)
session.assistant: The most recent plan is #8863. Dispatching it for remote implementation.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Dispatch plan #8863 for remote AI implementation

session.tool_result:
Checking GitHub authentication... Syncing trunk with remote... Resolving GitHub username... Validating 1 planned-PR plan(s)...
Validating PR #8863...
✓ All 1 plan(s) validated
#8863: [erk-plan] Fix: `erk pr teleport` doesn't register branch with Graphite for non-stacked PRs
Dispatching PR #8863...
Fetching plan content... Syncing branch: plnd/fix-teleport-graphite-trac-03-06-1432 Committing plan to branch... ✓ Branch pushed to remote
Dispatching workflow: plan-implement.yml Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) ✓ Workflow dispatched. ✓ Dispatch metadata written Posting queued event comment... ✓ Queued event comment posted
✓ 1 plan(s) dispatched successfully!
Dispatched plans:
... [4 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Clear plan-saved marker to allow new plans

session.assistant:
Plan #8863 dispatched successfully.
- **Plan**: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8863 - **Workflow**: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22784767393
The remote agent will implement the fix (restructuring `_reconstruct_graphite_stack` to always register branches with Graphite after teleport).
session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
